message.content:
You are analyzing a project-management web application located at D:\repos\poppim-web (freshly cloned from github.com/u2giants/poppim-web). Produce a DETAILED, EVIDENCE-BACKED technical report. Cite file paths. Do NOT modify any files — read only.

Context: This app ("poppim") is a project-management system for a home-decor design company that runs two product lines — "POP Creations" (LICENSED products needing licensor approval, e.g. Disney/Marvel) and "Spruce Line" (generic/original). It is intended to replace ClickUp and (per design docs) is "Directus-backed." Verify what it actually is from the code.

Investigate and report on:

1. STACK & SHAPE: What is the tech stack (framework, language, backend, DB/Directus, hosting hints like Cloudflare Workers/D1)? Is it a frontend, a backend, a Directus instance/config, schema/migrations, or a full app? Read package.json(s), README, top-level dirs, config. Is Directus actually used (look for directus config, collections, snapshot/schema files, migrations)?

2. DATA MODEL / SCHEMA — MOST IMPORTANT: Find and enumerate the data model. Look for Directus schema snapshots, SQL migrations, collection definitions, TypeScript types/interfaces, or D1 schema. List every collection/table/entity and its key fields. Especially identify entities for: project/offer, product/SKU, design, design collection, submission (licensor submission), Brand Assurance, sample/PPS, stage/stage_history, retailer/buyer, factory/vendor, licensor/property, season, revision/notes, order history, costing/pricing. Give the real names and fields as found in code.

3. LICENSING / PRODUCT TRACKING: How does poppim track the licensed-product approval process specifically? Is there a submission/approval/Brand Assurance/PPS concept? How are licensor, property, style guide, approval status, submission numbers modeled? How are products/SKUs and projects linked (parent-child)? How is stage/lifecycle status represented?

4. FEATURES & STATE: What features exist today vs scaffolding/planned (boards/kanban, role-based views, dashboards, AI assistant, flows/automations)? What roles/permissions exist? How complete is it? Look at commit history briefly (git log --oneline -30) and any docs/ folder.

5. OVERLAP SURFACE: Based on the schema, identify the concrete overlap with a separate "licensing tracking" grid in another app (DesignFlow PLM) that tracks: licensor submissions, products/SKUs, approval status, sample/PPS status, Brand Assurance numbers, factory/vendor, costing/pricing per product. Where would the same product/submission data live in poppim? What fields would map?

Be concrete and exhaustive on the data model and how licensing/products are tracked — that is the most important part. Return your findings as a structured markdown report. Run `git -C D:\repos\poppim-web log --oneline -30` and explore docs/ if present.

- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# AGENTS.md — poppim-web
2	
3	Canonical operating guide for **poppim-web**. Read this first; it routes you to everything else.
4	
5	## 1. Project summary
6	`poppim-web` is the **PIM (product/project management) frontend** for POP Creations — a React single-page app that is the human UI for the product-development pipeline, replacing the ClickUp board. **Users:** internal staff (designers, sales, licensing, management). It stores **no data of its own**; every read/write goes through the **shared Directus backend** at `https://data.designflow.app` (backend repo: `u2giants/directus`). The outcome that matters: a fast, tailored Kanban + task-detail app on the company's shared "super-app" database, so PIM data interlinks with the future CRM/DAM. Sibling frontends (separate repos): `popcmr-web` (CRM), `popdam-web` (DAM).
7	
8	**Live in production:** `https://pm.designflow.app` (the permanent human URL; admins use `data.designflow.app` for Directus Data Studio). Preview aliases `pm-dev` and `pm-ci` serve the same Coolify service. Deploy via `git push main` — see §13 and `docs/cicd.md`.
9	
10	## Multi-model AI note
11	
12	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
13	
14	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
15	
16	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
17	
18	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
19	
20	Always start with:
21	
22	- `AGENTS.md`
23	
24	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
25	
26	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
27	|---|---|---|
28	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | `docs/` deep dives |
29	| Modify a screen / app behavior (board, task detail, login) | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy changes |
30	| Add/change env vars, config, the backend URL | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if runtime/CORS affected | architecture docs |
31	| Change local setup, scripts, lint, shadcn components, tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md` | `docs/deployment.md` |
32	| Change Docker, hosting, the deploy/preview flow, rollback | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` | local-only dev docs |
33	| Change CI/CD, the release pipeline, GitHub Actions, registry, deploy trigger | `AGENTS.md`, **`docs/cicd.md`**, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | local-only dev docs |
34	| Change how data is read/written (collections, fields, the SDK) | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, **the `directus` repo's `AGENTS.md`** (backend schema is there) | deployment docs |
35	| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` §11 + §14, `HANDOFF.md` if present | unrelated docs |
36	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md` §15, **`HANDOFF.md`** (required reading when present) | docs outside the handoff scope |
37	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless needed |
38	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/` | source except to verify accuracy |
39	
40	## 4. Repository structure
41	
42	| Path | What | Ownership |
43	|---|---|---|
44	| `src/lib/` | `directus.ts` (SDK client + SSO helper), `types.ts` (typed slice of backend schema), `utils.ts` (`cn`, shadcn-generated) | owned (`utils.ts` generated) |
45	| `src/auth/auth.tsx` | `AuthProvider` + `useAuth()` — session check, login, SSO redirect, logout | owned |
46	| `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` | login screen | owned |
47	| `src/components/AppShell.tsx` | top bar + user menu | owned |
48	| `src/components/ui/` | **shadcn/ui components — GENERATED** (Radix/new-york). Re-add/update via `npx shadcn@latest add <name>`; hand-edits get overwritten | generated (vendored) |
49	| `src/features/board/` | the app's substance: `BoardPage`, `TaskCard`, `TaskDetailSheet`, `Collaboration.tsx`, `api.ts`, `collab.ts`, `stageColor.ts` | owned |
50	| `src/App.tsx`, `src/main.tsx`, `src/index.css` | root gate/providers, entry, theme tokens (`index.css` holds the Design-provided OKLCH theme) | owned |
51	| `index.html`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `components.json` | build/tooling config | owned |
52	| `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `.dockerignore` | container build (multi-stage node→nginx, SPA fallback) | owned |
53	| `.env`, `.env.example` | `VITE_DIRECTUS_URL` (not secret) | owned |
54	| `src/assets/pop-logo.png` | app logo used in Topbar | owned |
55	| `node_modules/`, `dist/` | install / build artifacts | ignore (§10) |
56	
57	No third-party framework source is vendored or modified (React/Radix/etc. are npm deps).
58	
59	## 5. Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
60	
61	Our custom code lives here:
62	
63	- `src/features/` — app features (the board + task detail)
64	- `src/auth/`, `src/pages/`, `src/components/AppShell.tsx` — app shell + auth
65	- `src/lib/directus.ts`, `src/lib/types.ts` — backend access
66	- `src/index.css` — theme tokens (from Claude Design)
67	- `docs/`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`
68	- `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, future `.github/workflows/`
69	
70	Everything else requires justification before touching — especially **`src/components/ui/` (generated shadcn)**: change those via the shadcn CLI, not by hand, or upgrades overwrite your edits.
71	
72	## 6. Core modification inventory
73	
74	No files **outside project-owned areas** were modified — no third-party/framework source is vendored here. The only "generated" code in-tree is the shadcn `src/components/ui/*` + `src/lib/utils.ts`, managed by the shadcn CLI.
75	
76	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
77	|---|---|---|---|
78	| `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.app.json` | Added `paths` `@/*` → `src/*` (no `baseUrl` — deprecated in TS7) | shadcn/import alias | re-check if shadcn CLI rewrites tsconfig |
79	| `vite.config.ts` | Added `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin + `@` alias | Tailwind v4 + alias | low |
80	| `components.json` | `style: "new-york"` (Radix), not the CLI default `base-nova` (Base UI) | see §11 | re-adding components uses this style |
81	
82	## 7. Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes
83	
84	| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
85	|---|---|---|
86	| Change the board (columns, cards, drag) | `src/features/board/BoardPage.tsx`, `TaskCard.tsx`, `stageColor.ts` | `src/components/ui/*` by hand |
87	| Change the task-detail panel | `src/features/board/TaskDetailSheet.tsx`, `Collaboration.tsx` | — |
88	| Change what data is fetched/written | `src/features/board/api.ts`, `collab.ts`, `src/lib/types.ts` | the backend schema (edit in the `directus` repo) |
89	| Change auth/login | `src/auth/auth.tsx`, `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`, `src/lib/directus.ts` | — |
90	| Add a shadcn component | `npx shadcn@latest add <name>` (writes `src/components/ui/`) | hand-writing UI primitives |
91	| Change brand theme/tokens | `src/index.css` (`:root`/`.dark` OKLCH blocks) | component files for colors |
92	| Add an env var | `.env.example`, `src/lib/directus.ts`, `docs/configuration.md` | committing real `.env` |
93	| Change the deploy/container | `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `docs/deployment.md` | prod containers directly |
94	
95	## 8. Data model and external identifiers
96	
97	This app only **reads/writes** the backend; the schema is defined in the `directus` repo. Identifiers it depends on:
98	
99	| Entity/System | Identifier | Where defined | Notes |
100	|---|---|---|---|
101	| Backend API | `https://data.designflow.app` | `src/lib/directus.ts` (`VITE_DIRECTUS_URL`) | shared Directus; **never** the human URL |
102	| This app (production) | `https://pm.designflow.app` | Coolify service `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y` (GHCR image) | **live** — permanent human URL |
103	| This app (aliases) | `https://pm-dev.designflow.app`, `https://pm-ci.designflow.app` | same Coolify service | preview / CI-validation |
104	| GHCR image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web` | GitHub Actions | **public** package; tags `:main` + `:sha-<commit>` |
105	| Repo | `u2giants/poppim-web` | GitHub | |
106	| Container | `poppim-web` | raw `docker run` on the `coolify` network | §9 |
107	| Backend collections read | `product`, `stage`, `retailer` | `directus` repo `pm-system/apply-schema.mjs` | board data |
108	| Collaboration collections | `checklist_item`, `subtask`, `product_assignee` (M2M), native `directus_comments` | `directus` repo `pm-system/add-collaboration-model.mjs` | task-detail |
109	| Image field | `product.cover_url` | `directus` repo `pm-system/migration/clickup-images.mjs` | public ClickUp CDN URL (see §11) |
110	
111	Do not rename these identifiers casually — both repos depend on them.
112	
113	## 9. Container and service inventory
114	
115	| Container/service | Purpose | Managed by | App/project ID | Image/source |
116	|---|---|---|---|---|
117	| `poppim-web-ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y` | This frontend (prod) — pulls the CI image | **Coolify** | service uuid `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`, project `jdq36h5dq74o6ddhich9l796` | `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web:main` |
118	| `directus-app` / `directus-db` | The backend this app calls | Coolify service `directus` (`nzli85mk3luzb6u7cnq5fidu`) | see `directus` repo | `directus/directus:11` + `postgres:16-alpine` |
119	
120	## 10. What to ignore
121	
122	Do not load these into AI context: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.env`, `*.local`, `.cache/`, `coverage/`. Matches `.claudeignore` / `.cursorignore`.
123	
124	## 11. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
125	
126	### shadcn uses the `new-york` (Radix) style, not the CLI default
127	Looks like: `components.json` has `style: "new-york"` and components import from `radix-ui`, even though `npx shadcn init` now defaults to `base-nova` (Base UI).
128	Actually: we deliberately switched to the Radix-based `new-york` style.
129	Why: Base UI uses a `render` prop instead of Radix's `asChild`, and most shadcn docs/examples assume Radix — for an AI-assisted, docs-driven workflow that mismatch caused friction (and an unused-`React`-import build error).
130	Do not change because: reverting to Base UI breaks every `asChild` usage and diverges from the examples future sessions will copy.
131	
132	### Auth is session/cookie mode, not token mode
133	Looks like: `directus.ts` uses `authentication('session', { credentials: 'include' })` and there's no token in `localStorage`.
134	Actually: the frontend (`pm-dev`/`pm`) and API (`data`) are sibling subdomains, so auth uses a session cookie scoped to `.designflow.app`. This is what makes Microsoft SSO return into the SPA.
135	Why: cross-subdomain SSO needs the cookie domain + CORS credentials set on the backend (see `directus` repo AGENTS.md §11).
136	Do not change because: switching to token/`json` mode breaks SSO return and the cross-subdomain session.
137	
138	### `TaskCard` needs `shrink-0`
139	Looks like: a stray `shrink-0` on the card.
140	Actually: cards are flex children in a fixed-height column; without `shrink-0` they get squeezed by flex-shrink to ~2px and the column won't scroll.
141	Why/Do not change: removing it collapses every card (this exact bug shipped once — see §14).
142	
143	### `cover_url` points at ClickUp's CDN (not our storage)
144	Looks like: product images load from `*.clickup-attachments.com`.
145	Actually: the importer stores ClickUp's public thumbnail URL directly on `product.cover_url` (no download/storage).
146	Why: fast, no storage volume needed for the preview.
147	Do not change without: knowing these URLs die if ClickUp is cancelled — durable storage (copy to DAM/R2) is a planned follow-up (§15).
148	
149	### Pipeline filters are server-side (Directus API)
150	Search and licensor filters are pushed to Directus via `_icontains` / `name._in`. The pipeline loads 300 products unfiltered, 500 when any filter is active. A parallel `aggregate` count query surfaces the true total; a toast / table footer shows "Showing first N of M" when truncated. Search is debounced 300 ms; stale fetch results are discarded via an incrementing ref.
151	Do not assume: all 15K+ products are loaded at once — they aren't.
152	
153	### No client-side router
154	The app uses a simple auth gate in `App.tsx`, not routes. Deep-linking is done with `history.replaceState` + `URLSearchParams` (`?item=<uuid>`). `react-router-dom` is installed but not used — don't add route components without a clear reason.
155	
156	## 12. Credentials and environment
157	
158	The frontend holds **no secrets** (it's a browser app; all auth is via the backend session cookie).
159	
160	| Variable | Purpose | Stored where | Required in dev | Required in prod |
161	|---|---|---|---|---|
162	| `VITE_DIRECTUS_URL` | Backend API base URL (build-time) | `.env` / `.env.example` (defaults to `https://data.designflow.app` in `src/lib/directus.ts`) | optional | optional (baked at build) |
163	| `VITE_BUILD_GIT_SHA`, `VITE_BUILD_COMMIT_DATE`, `VITE_BUILD_RUN` | Build metadata displayed in the top bar and used for deploy verification | Set by Docker build args in CI; local fallback in `vite.config.ts` | no | set by workflow |
164	
165	**Backend-side env this app depends on** (set on the `directus` Coolify service, NOT here): this app's origin must be in `CORS_ORIGIN` and `AUTH_MICROSOFT_REDIRECT_ALLOW_LIST`, with `CORS_CREDENTIALS=true` and the `.designflow.app` session-cookie settings. See `directus` repo AGENTS.md §11/§12.
166	
167	## 13. Deployment
168	
169	**LIVE pipeline (the only deploy path — see `docs/cicd.md`):** push to `main` → GitHub Actions (`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`) verify → build → push `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web:main`+`:sha-<commit>` → trigger Coolify (service `poppim-web`, uuid `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`) → Coolify pulls + runs. Actions never touches the server. **A `git push` to `main` is the entire deploy.** The GHCR package is **public**, so Coolify pulls anonymously (no registry cred).
170	
171	- **Production:** `pm.designflow.app` is served by the Coolify service (container `poppim-web-ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`); `pm-dev`/`pm-ci` point at the same service. Domains bound via the Coolify sub-app `fqdn` (`service_applications` id=17).
172	- **Rollback:** redeploy a prior `:sha-<commit>` image tag via Coolify (`docs/cicd.md`).
173	- **Retired:** the legacy raw-`docker run` deploy was removed at cutover (2026-06-11). `docs/deployment.md` documents it for history only — do not reintroduce raw docker.
174	- Data Studio (Directus) is at `data.designflow.app`; this app is the human URL `pm.designflow.app`.
175	- **Runtime env:** `VITE_*` is **baked at build time** (static SPA) — there is no runtime env to change; rebuild to change the backend URL.
176	- **§QUIRK-1 — service vs application:** `poppim-web` is a Coolify *service* (docker-compose), not a Coolify *application*. The alternative `/api/v1/deploy?uuid=` endpoint silently no-ops on services (returns HTTP 200, does nothing). See `docs/cicd.md §QUIRK-1`.
177	- **§QUIRK-3 — restart does NOT pull `:main`:** `GET /services/{uuid}/restart` reuses the locally-cached image, so pushing a new `:main` and restarting kept serving the OLD bundle (this caused a full day of "my changes aren't live"). The workflow now `PATCH`es the service `docker_compose_raw` (base64-encoded — plain text returns 422) to the immutable `:sha-<commit>` tag, **then** restarts; a never-cached SHA tag forces a GHCR pull. Do not revert to restart-on-`:main`. See `docs/cicd.md §QUIRK-3`.
178	- **§QUIRK-2 — Caddy intercepts `/version.json`:** Coolify's Caddy layer applies `try_files` before nginx, so `https://pm.designflow.app/version.json` returns the SPA shell — it cannot be polled to confirm a deploy. The CI verify step checks the `build-sha` meta tag in the served HTML instead. See `docs/cicd.md §QUIRK-2`.
179	- **Top-bar build badge:** `src/components/Topbar.tsx` displays the short commit SHA and commit timestamp in `America/New_York`, sourced from `src/lib/buildInfo.ts`. Do not turn this into runtime config; it is intentionally baked into the static build for auditability.
180	
181	## 14. Critical incidents
182	
183	### 2026-06-11 — Cards collapsed to 2px on the board
184	What happened: after the Prompt-B board rework, all task cards rendered ~2px tall (only the border), content hidden.
185	Impact: board unusable (caught in preview before anyone relied on it).
186	Root cause: cards are flex children in a fixed-height, scrollable column; default `flex-shrink:1` compressed them to fit instead of overflowing.
187	Recovery: added `shrink-0` to `TaskCard` so cards keep natural height and the column scrolls.
188	Rule added: see §11 "TaskCard needs `shrink-0`" — don't remove it.
189	
190	### 2026-06-11 — Image importer crashed (backend, but affected this app's images)
191	What happened: the ClickUp image importer (in the `directus` repo) died at ~800 products; image counts stalled.
192	Root cause: its periodic re-login sent the already-expired token, so the re-login itself 401'd.
193	Recovery: fixed to clear the token before re-login + retry; documented here because this app surfaces those images.
194	
195	No production incidents (the app is preview-only so far).
196	
197	## 15. Pending work

199	| Status | Item | Owner/next action |
200	|---|---|---|
201	| in-progress | ClickUp image backfill | Importer running in the `directus` repo (`pm-system/migration/clickup-images.mjs`); ~thousands remaining, fills `product.cover_url` |
202	| done | CI/Coolify pipeline + cutover | `git push main` → Actions → GHCR → Coolify (`ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`) serving `pm.designflow.app`; legacy raw-docker removed; GHCR package public; 2026-06-11. See `docs/cicd.md`. |
203	| done | Server-side filtering/pagination | Search + licensor pushed to Directus `_icontains`/`_in`; parallel count query; debounced 300ms; table prev/next pagination; truncation badge; 2026-06-12 |
204	| done | Wire PipelinePage to real Directus data | `pipeline/adapter.ts` + `pipeline/api.ts`; real products/stages/licensors; drag-to-stage with optimistic updates; 2026-06-12 |
205	| done | Wire TaskDetailModal to real Directus data | Comments and assignees loaded from Directus via `board/collab.ts`; mock comments removed; 2026-06-12 |
206	| done | Cover images on PimTaskCard | `cover_url` fetched and rendered as top banner when present; 2026-06-12 |
207	| done | Delete leftover Vite-template assets + dead board files | `src/assets/*`, `public/icons.svg`, and 7 unreachable `features/board/` files removed; 2026-06-12 |
208	| done | Production deploy at `pm.designflow.app` | live via Coolify service `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`; SSO + cert verified; raw-docker retired 2026-06-11 |
209	| open | List / Timeline views | Table view exists; Timeline tab is a placeholder |

1	import { readItems, aggregate } from '@directus/sdk'
2	import { directus } from '@/lib/directus'
3	import type { Product, Stage } from '@/lib/types'
4	import { LICENSOR_DISPLAY } from './adapter'
5	export { fetchStages, setProductStage, stageId } from '../board/api'
6	
7	const PIPELINE_FIELDS = [
8	  'id',
9	  'code',
10	  'name',
11	  'description',
12	  'priority',
13	  'on_shelf_date',
14	  'pi_status',
15	  'cover_url',
16	  'clickup_url',
17	  'clickup_list_name',
18	  'clickup_created_at',
19	  'clickup_updated_at',
20	  'clickup_closed_at',
21	  'clickup_start_at',
22	  'clickup_due_at',
23	  { stage: ['id', 'name'] },
24	  { licensor: ['id', 'name'] },
25	] as const
26	
27	// Maps display-name licensors (e.g. "Disney") back to the raw names stored in Directus ("disney")
28	function toRawLicensors(displayNames: string[]): string[] {
29	  return Object.entries(LICENSOR_DISPLAY)
30	    .filter(([, display]) => displayNames.includes(display))
31	    .map(([raw]) => raw)
32	}
33	
34	function buildFilter(search?: string, licensors?: string[]): Record<string, unknown> {
35	  const raw = licensors?.length ? toRawLicensors(licensors) : []
36	  const and: unknown[] = [{ stage: { _nnull: true } }]
37	  if (search?.trim()) {
38	    and.push({ _or: [{ name: { _icontains: search.trim() } }, { code: { _icontains: search.trim() } }] })
39	  }
40	  if (raw.length) and.push({ licensor: { name: { _in: raw } } })
41	  return { _and: and }
42	}
43	
44	export interface FetchProductsOpts {
45	  search?: string
46	  licensors?: string[]
47	  limit?: number
48	}
49	
50	export async function fetchPipelineProducts(opts: FetchProductsOpts = {}): Promise<Product[]> {
51	  const { search, licensors, limit = 300 } = opts
52	  return directus.request(
53	    readItems('product', {
54	      fields: PIPELINE_FIELDS,
55	      filter: buildFilter(search, licensors) as never,
56	      limit,
57	    }),
58	  ) as Promise<Product[]>
59	}
60	
61	export async function countPipelineProducts(opts: Omit<FetchProductsOpts, 'limit'> = {}): Promise<number> {
62	  const result = await directus.request(
63	    aggregate('product', {
64	      aggregate: { count: '*' },
65	      filter: buildFilter(opts.search, opts.licensors) as never,
66	    }),
67	  ) as Array<{ count: { '*': string } }>
68	  return parseInt(result[0]?.count?.['*'] ?? '0', 10)
69	}
70	
71	export function stageById(stages: Stage[]): Map<string, Stage> {
72	  return new Map(stages.map((s) => [s.id, s]))
73	}
74

1	// Converts real Directus Product + Stage data to the display shape PimTaskCard expects.
2	
3	import type { Product, Stage } from '@/lib/types'
4	import type { MockTask, Category } from '@/lib/mockData'
5	
6	// ─── Licensor normalisation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
7	
8	export const LICENSOR_DISPLAY: Record<string, string> = {
9	  disney: 'Disney',
10	  marvel: 'Marvel',
11	  'star wars': 'Star Wars',
12	  wb: 'WB',
13	  nbcu: 'NBCU',
14	  nick: 'Nickelodeon',
15	  nickelodeon: 'Nickelodeon',
16	  sanrio: 'Sanrio',
17	  peanuts: 'Peanuts',
18	  sega: 'Sega',
19	  wwe: 'WWE',
20	  'care bears': 'Care Bears',
21	  'coca cola': 'Coca-Cola',
22	  'one piece': 'One Piece',
23	  'sesame street': 'Sesame Street',
24	  'strawberry shortcake': 'Strawberry SC',
25	  lucasfilm: 'Lucasfilm',
26	  seasonal: 'Seasonal',
27	}
28	
29	function normaliseLicensor(raw: string | null | undefined): string {
30	  if (!raw) return 'Unknown'
31	  const key = raw.trim().toLowerCase()
32	  return LICENSOR_DISPLAY[key] ?? raw.trim().replace(/\b\w/g, (c) => c.toUpperCase())
33	}
34	
35	// ─── Category inference ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	const CATEGORY_KEYWORDS: Array<[RegExp, Category]> = [
38	  [/canvas|frame|art|print|poster|picture|sign|decal/i, 'frames'],
39	  [/plush|stuffed|plushie/i, 'plush'],
40	  [/candle/i, 'candle'],
41	  [/lamp|light|luminara|lantern|night\s*light/i, 'lighting'],
42	  [/mug|cup|apron|plate|bowl|kitchen|utensil|towel(?=.*kitchen)|trivet/i, 'kitchen'],
43	  [/bath|shower|mat(?=.*bath)|loofa/i, 'bath'],
44	  [/pillow|throw|blanket|tapestry|textile|quilt|bedding|towel(?!.*kitchen)/i, 'textiles'],
45	  [/ornament|holiday|christmas|seasonal|wreath/i, 'seasonal'],
46	  [/storage|bin|basket|box|bag|organizer/i, 'storage'],
47	]
48	
49	function inferCategory(name: string): Category {
50	  for (const [re, cat] of CATEGORY_KEYWORDS) {
51	    if (re.test(name)) return cat
52	  }
53	  return 'storage'
54	}
55	
56	// ─── Stage colour hashing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57	// Assigns one of 8 deterministic palette slots by hashing the stage name.
58	
59	const STAGE_PALETTE: Array<{ bg: string; dot: string }> = [
60	  { bg: '#ECE2F8', dot: '#8B5CF6' }, // violet
61	  { bg: '#DEEBFB', dot: '#3B82F6' }, // blue
62	  { bg: '#D8EFF5', dot: '#0891B2' }, // cyan
63	  { bg: '#D8EFE7', dot: '#10B981' }, // emerald
64	  { bg: '#FBEBD3', dot: '#F59E0B' }, // amber
65	  { bg: '#FBE2D8', dot: '#EF4444' }, // red
66	  { bg: '#F7E5EC', dot: '#DB2777' }, // pink
67	  { bg: '#DEF0DB', dot: '#22C55E' }, // green
68	]
69	
70	function hashStr(s: string) {
71	  let h = 0
72	  for (let i = 0; i < s.length; i++) h = (h * 31 + s.charCodeAt(i)) >>> 0
73	  return h
74	}
75	
76	export function stageColor(stageName: string): { bg: string; dot: string } {
77	  return STAGE_PALETTE[hashStr(stageName) % STAGE_PALETTE.length]
78	}
79	
80	// ─── Name cleanup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
81	// ClickUp-imported names may have a catalog code + tab prepended: "MTP78\tReal name"
82	
83	function cleanName(raw: string | null, code: string | null): string {
84	  if (!raw) return code ?? 'Untitled'
85	  const tabIdx = raw.indexOf('\t')
86	  return tabIdx !== -1 ? raw.slice(tabIdx + 1).trim() : raw.trim()
87	}
88	
89	// ─── Due date formatting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	
91	function formatDue(iso: string | null): { due: string | null; dueOver: boolean } {
92	  if (!iso) return { due: null, dueOver: false }
93	  const delta = Math.ceil((new Date(iso).getTime() - Date.now()) / 86_400_000)
94	  if (delta < 0) return { due: 'Overdue', dueOver: true }
95	  if (delta === 0) return { due: 'Today', dueOver: false }
96	  if (delta === 1) return { due: '1d left', dueOver: false }
97	  if (delta < 30) return { due: `${delta}d left`, dueOver: false }
98	  return {
99	    due: new Date(iso).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }),
100	    dueOver: false,
101	  }
102	}
103	
104	// ─── Pill mapping ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
105	
106	function piStatusPill(pi: string | null): string | null {
107	  if (!pi) return null
108	  if (pi === 'Required') return 'PI Req'
109	  if (pi === 'Completed') return null
110	  return null
111	}
112	
113	function normalisePriority(priority: string | null): MockTask['priority'] {
114	  const p = (priority ?? '').toLowerCase()
115	  if (p.includes('urgent')) return 'urgent'
116	  if (p.includes('high')) return 'high'
117	  if (p.includes('low')) return 'low'
118	  return 'normal'
119	}
120	
121	// ─── Main converter ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	
123	export function productToTask(product: Product): MockTask {
124	  const licensorRaw =
125	    typeof product.licensor === 'object' && product.licensor !== null
126	      ? product.licensor.name
127	      : null
128	  const licensor = normaliseLicensor(licensorRaw)
129	  const stageRaw =
130	    typeof product.stage === 'object' && product.stage !== null
131	      ? product.stage.name
132	      : typeof product.stage === 'string'
133	        ? product.stage
134	        : 'Unknown'
135	  const title = cleanName(product.name, product.code)
136	  const { due, dueOver } = formatDue(product.clickup_due_at ?? product.on_shelf_date)
137	
138	  return {
139	    id: product.id,
140	    stage: stageRaw,
141	    title,
142	    licensor,
143	    category: inferCategory(title),
144	    priority: normalisePriority(product.priority),
145	    time: '',
146	    due,
147	    dueOver,
148	    checklist: { done: 0, total: 0 },
149	    comments: 0,
150	    attach: 0,
151	    pill: piStatusPill(product.pi_status),
152	    assignees: [],
153	    coverUrl: product.cover_url ?? undefined,
154	    coverThumbUrl: thumbUrl(product.cover_url, product.id),
155	    description: product.description ?? undefined,
156	    clickupUrl: product.clickup_url ?? undefined,
157	    clickupListName: product.clickup_list_name ?? undefined,
158	    clickupCreatedAt: product.clickup_created_at ?? undefined,
159	    clickupUpdatedAt: product.clickup_updated_at ?? undefined,
160	    clickupClosedAt: product.clickup_closed_at ?? undefined,
161	    clickupStartAt: product.clickup_start_at ?? undefined,
162	    clickupDueAt: product.clickup_due_at ?? undefined,
163	  }
164	}
165	
166	// Covers stored on DigitalOcean Spaces have a companion thumbnail at
167	// covers/<id>_thumb.webp (board cards use the thumb; the opened card uses the
168	// full original). For covers still on ClickUp's CDN (mid-migration) there is no
169	// thumb, so the card falls back to the full URL.
170	const SPACES_COVERS = 'https://poppim.nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/covers/'
171	function thumbUrl(coverUrl: string | null, id: string): string | undefined {
172	  if (!coverUrl) return undefined
173	  if (coverUrl.startsWith(SPACES_COVERS)) return `${SPACES_COVERS}${id}_thumb.webp`
174	  return coverUrl
175	}
176	
177	// ─── Stage list ordering ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
178	// Builds the ordered list of stage names from fetched Stage objects.
179	
180	export function orderedStageNames(stages: Stage[]): string[] {
181	  const sorted = [...stages].sort((a, b) => (a.stage_order ?? 0) - (b.stage_order ?? 0))
182	  const seen = new Set<string>()
183	  return sorted.map((s) => s.name).filter((n) => (seen.has(n) ? false : (seen.add(n), true)))
184	}

1	# Architecture — poppim-web
2	
3	See `AGENTS.md` first. This doc covers system design and data flow.
4	
5	## Shape
6	A **React 19 + Vite SPA** that is a pure client of the shared **Directus** API. It holds no data and no business database — Directus owns the schema, auth, permissions, and storage.
7	
8	```
9	Browser (pm-dev / pm.designflow.app)
10	  └─ poppim-web (this repo, static SPA served by nginx)
11	        └─ HTTPS + session cookie ──▶ data.designflow.app  (Directus API)
12	                                          └─ Postgres (shared: PIM + future CRM/DAM)
13	```
14	
15	## Stack
16	- React 19 + Vite + TypeScript (strict).
17	- Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui (`new-york`/Radix — see AGENTS.md §11).
18	- `@directus/sdk` for all data + auth.
19	- `@dnd-kit/core` for board drag.
20	- `lucide-react` icons; `sonner` toasts; `@fontsource-variable/geist` font.
21	- Path alias `@/*` → `src/*`.
22	
23	## Data flow
24	- **Client:** `src/lib/directus.ts` builds the SDK client (`authentication('session', {credentials:'include'})` + `rest`). `DIRECTUS_URL` = `VITE_DIRECTUS_URL` or the hardcoded prod default.
25	- **Types:** `src/lib/types.ts` is a hand-maintained typed slice of the backend collections this app reads.
26	- **Board:** `src/features/board/api.ts` fetches `stage` (sorted) + `product` (capped page, with `cover_url`). `BoardPage` groups products by stage into droppable columns; `@dnd-kit` drag → `setProductStage` (optimistic, reverts on error).
27	- **Task detail:** `TaskDetailSheet` (720px two-column slide-over) + `Collaboration.tsx` + `collab.ts` read/write `checklist_item`, `subtask`, `product_assignee` (M2M), and native `directus_comments`.
28	- **Auth:** `src/auth/auth.tsx` (`AuthProvider`/`useAuth`) checks the session via `readMe`, supports email/password `login` and a Microsoft SSO redirect (`microsoftLoginUrl` → backend `/auth/login/microsoft`). `App.tsx` gates: loading → `LoginPage` → `AppShell` + `BoardPage`.
29	- **Build metadata:** CI passes the commit SHA, commit timestamp, and GitHub run id into the Vite build. `src/lib/buildInfo.ts` exposes that metadata, `Topbar` displays the short SHA + New York commit time, and `index.html` embeds `build-sha` for production deploy verification.
30	
31	## Constraints
32	- **Cross-subdomain auth:** depends on backend session-cookie + CORS config (AGENTS.md §11/§12); the frontend can't fix auth alone.
33	- **Backend is the source of truth:** to add a field/collection, change the **`directus`** repo schema first, then surface it here.
34	- **Static build:** `VITE_*` is baked at build time; no runtime config. Build metadata is intentionally static, not fetched from Directus.
35	
36	## Where the backend lives
37	Backend schema, roles, SSO, and migrations are in `u2giants/directus` (`pm-system/`). Read that repo's `AGENTS.md` for collections, the role model, and the domain plan.
38

2	import { directus } from '@/lib/directus'
3	import type { Product, Stage } from '@/lib/types'
4	
5	export async function setProductStage(productId: string, stageId: string | null) {
6	  return directus.request(updateItem('product', productId, { stage: stageId }))
7	}
8	
9	export async function fetchStages(): Promise<Stage[]> {
10	  return directus.request(
11	    readItems('stage', { sort: ['stage_order'], limit: -1 }),
12	  ) as Promise<Stage[]>
13	}

15	// First slice loads a capped page of products; filtering/pagination comes next.
16	export async function fetchProducts(limit = 500): Promise<Product[]> {
17	  return directus.request(
18	    readItems('product', {
19	      fields: [
20	        'id',
21	        'code',
22	        'name',
23	        'business_unit',
24	        'on_shelf_date',
25	        'pi_status',
26	        'cover_url',
27	        { stage: ['id', 'name'] },
28	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
29	        { licensor: ['id', 'name'] },
30	      ],
31	      limit,
32	    }),
33	  ) as Promise<Product[]>
34	}
35	
36	// Assignee membership for the loaded products (sparse), for the Assignee filter.
37	export async function fetchAssigneeMap() {
38	  const rows = (await directus.request(
39	    readItems('product_assignee', {
40	      fields: ['product', { directus_user: ['id'] }] as any,
41	      limit: -1,
42	    }),
43	  )) as unknown as Array<{ product: string; directus_user: { id: string } | string }>
44	  const map = new Map<string, Set<string>>()
45	  for (const r of rows) {
46	    const uid = typeof r.directus_user === 'string' ? r.directus_user : r.directus_user?.id
47	    if (!r.product || !uid) continue
48	    if (!map.has(r.product)) map.set(r.product, new Set())
49	    map.get(r.product)!.add(uid)
50	  }
51	  return map
52	}
53	
54	export function stageId(p: Product): string | null {
55	  if (!p.stage) return null
56	  return typeof p.stage === 'string' ? p.stage : p.stage.id
57	}

1	import { readItems, aggregate } from '@directus/sdk'
2	import { directus } from '@/lib/directus'
3	import type { Project, Product } from '@/lib/types'
4	
5	export async function fetchProjects(): Promise<Project[]> {
6	  return directus.request(
7	    readItems('project', {
8	      fields: ['id', 'title', 'status', 'business_unit', 'on_shelf_date', 'brief', 'restrictions',
9	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] }],
10	      sort: ['title'],
11	      limit: -1,
12	    }),
13	  ) as Promise<Project[]>
14	}
15	
16	// Returns a map of projectId → product count (single query via groupBy).
17	export async function fetchProjectProductCounts(): Promise<Map<string, number>> {
18	  const rows = (await directus.request(
19	    aggregate('product', {
20	      aggregate: { count: 'id' },
21	      groupBy: ['project'] as never,
22	      filter: { project: { _nnull: true } } as never,
23	    }),
24	  )) as unknown as Array<{ project: string; count: { id: string } }>
25	  return new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.project, parseInt(r.count.id, 10)]))
26	}
27	
28	// Fetches the products belonging to a single project.
29	export async function fetchProjectProducts(projectId: string): Promise<Product[]> {
30	  return directus.request(
31	    readItems('product', {
32	      fields: ['id', 'code', 'name', 'on_shelf_date', 'pi_status', 'cover_url',
33	        { stage: ['id', 'name'] }, { licensor: ['id', 'name'] }],
34	      filter: { project: { _eq: projectId } } as never,
35	      limit: 200,
36	      sort: ['name'],
37	    }),
38	  ) as Promise<Product[]>
39	}

content.text:
I have everything needed. The grep confirms there is **no** dedicated submission/Brand Assurance/PPS/factory/vendor/costing entity in poppim's schema — those terms appear only in mock note text and a few unused optional fields on `MockTask`. The nested `designflow-*` dirs don't exist in poppim-web (they were from a prior glob over `D:\repos\` — not part of this repo). Here is the report.

---

# Technical Report — poppim-web (POP Creations PIM)

## 1. Stack & Shape

**It is a frontend-only React SPA — a thin client of a shared Directus backend. It stores no data of its own and contains no schema, migrations, or Directus config.**

- **Framework / language:** React 19 + Vite 8 + TypeScript (strict, `noUnusedLocals`/`noUnusedParameters`). `package.json`: `"dev": "vite"`, `"build": "tsc -b && vite build"`.
- **UI:** Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui in the `new-york`/Radix style (`components.json`, `src/components/ui/*` are generated). `@dnd-kit/core` for board drag, `lucide-react` icons, `sonner` toasts, `next-themes`.
- **Backend access:** `@directus/sdk` v18 only. Client built in `src/lib/directus.ts` against `VITE_DIRECTUS_URL` (default hardcoded `https://data.designflow.app`). Auth is **session/cookie mode** (`authentication('session', { credentials:'include', autoRefresh:true })`) scoped to `.designflow.app`, enabling cross-subdomain Microsoft SSO via `GET {DIRECTUS_URL}/auth/login/microsoft?redirect=...`.
- **Hosting:** NOT Cloudflare Workers/D1. Built as a static SPA, containerized (`Dockerfile` multi-stage node→nginx, `nginx.conf` SPA fallback), pushed to `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web`, deployed to **Coolify** (service uuid `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`) at `pm.designflow.app` via GitHub Actions on push to `main` (`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `docs/cicd.md`). Images for product covers live on **DigitalOcean Spaces** (`poppim.nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/covers/`, `src/features/pipeline/adapter.ts`).

**Is Directus actually used?** Yes, as the runtime backend — every read/write goes through `@directus/sdk`. But **the Directus schema itself is not in this repo.** Per `AGENTS.md` §8 and `docs/architecture.md`, the collections/migrations live in a separate repo `u2giants/directus` (`pm-system/apply-schema.mjs`, `add-collaboration-model.mjs`). poppim-web only carries a **hand-maintained typed slice** of that schema in `src/lib/types.ts`. There is no `snapshot.yaml`, no SQL, no `directus.config`, no D1 here.

The backend Postgres is described (`docs/architecture.md`) as **shared across PIM + future CRM/DAM** — sibling frontends `popcrm-web` and `popdam-web` are separate repos. The `designflow-*` directories seen in an early glob are other repos under `D:\repos\`, not part of poppim-web.

## 2. Data Model / Schema

The schema poppim consumes is defined in `src/lib/types.ts` (the `Schema` interface, lines 173-188) plus native Directus `directus_users` and `directus_comments`. Collections and key fields:

**`product`** (the central entity — both "product/SKU" AND the pipeline "task"; there is no separate task table). Fields (`types.ts` 33-54):
`id, code, name, description, priority, business_unit, stage (M2O), retailer (M2O), licensor (M2O), project (M2O), on_shelf_date, pi_status, cover_url, clickup_url, clickup_list_name, clickup_created_at, clickup_updated_at, clickup_closed_at, clickup_start_at, clickup_due_at`. There are ~15,228 staged product rows (per `HANDOFF.md`).

**`project`** (the "offer"/collection grouping — 651 imported ClickUp records). Fields: `id, title, status, business_unit, retailer (M2O), on_shelf_date, brief, restrictions`.

**`stage`** (lifecycle/Kanban column). Fields: `id, name, stage_order, category, business_unit`.

**`retailer`** (buyer/customer): `id, name`.

**`licensor`** (the property/IP owner): `id, name`. Note: only `name` — no separate "property/style guide" entity.

**`stage`** is the only lifecycle model; product→stage is a single M2O pointer (`product.stage`). **There is no `stage_history` / status-transition log** — stage changes are a direct `updateItem('product', id, { stage })` (`board/api.ts setProductStage`).

**Collaboration collections** (added in the backend's `add-collaboration-model.mjs`):
- **`checklist_item`**: `id, product, label, done, sort, group_name, source_id, source_system`
- **`subtask`**: `id, product, title, done, assignee (M2O user), due_date, sort`
- **`product_assignee`** (M2M product↔directus_users): `id, product, directus_user`
- native **`directus_comments`** (filtered by `collection='product', item=<id>`)

**ClickUp-migration collections** (each M2O to `product`, surfaced in `TaskDetailModal`):
- **`product_file`**: `id, product, title, file_type, mime_type, size, source_url, thumbnail_url, stored_url, uploaded_at`
- **`product_update`**: `id, product, body, author_name, author_email, happened_at, kind`
- **`product_tag`**: `id, product, name, color`
- **`product_field`** (generic EAV for imported ClickUp custom fields): `id, product, name, field_type, value_text, value_json`
- **`product_activity`**: `id, product, action, detail, actor_name, happened_at`
- **`product_link`** (product↔product relations): `id, product, linked_product, linked_external_id, linked_title, relation_type, direction, created_by, created_at`
- **`product_time_entry`**: `id, product, user_name, user_email, started_at, ended_at, duration_ms, duration_hours, billable, description, tags`

**`directus_users`** (typed as `DirectusUser`): `id, first_name, last_name, email, avatar, role {id,name}`.

## 3. Licensing / Product Tracking

**poppim does NOT model a formal licensed-product approval process.** This is the most important finding. There is:

- **Licensor:** a flat `licensor` collection with only `id, name`. Display names are normalized client-side via a hardcoded map in `adapter.ts` (`LICENSOR_DISPLAY`: disney→Disney, marvel→Marvel, lucasfilm→Lucasfilm, sanrio, nbcu, wb, nick, peanuts, sega, wwe, care bears, coca cola, one piece, sesame street, strawberry shortcake, seasonal, star wars). `product.licensor` is the only product→licensor link.
- **No `property`, no `style_guide`, no `submission`, no `Brand Assurance`, no `PPS`/`sample` collection exists.** A grep across `src/` for `submission|brand assurance|PPS|approval|factory|vendor|costing|price|cost|style guide|property|sample|revision` returns only: (a) mock note prose in `mockData.ts` (e.g., "Licensor approval needed before sampling", "Factory hand-off"), and (b) unused optional fields `buyer?, factory?, retailer?, dueLicensor?` on the `MockTask` display interface (`mockData.ts` 90-93) that are populated on exactly one hardcoded mock card (t4) and never read from Directus.
- **Approval status is represented only as a `stage`.** The licensed-approval lifecycle is implied by stage *names* (mock `STAGES` = Concept → In Development → **Licensor Review** → **Approved** → Sampling → Production → Shipped, `mockData.ts:123`), but real stages come from the backend `stage` collection sorted by `stage_order`. There is no enum, no submission number, no approval-status field.
- **`pi_status`** (values "Required"/"Completed") is the only product-level status flag beyond stage; rendered as a "PI Req" pill (`adapter.ts piStatusPill`). "PI" appears to mean a packaging/product-information requirement, not licensor approval.
- **Product ↔ Project linkage:** `product.project` (M2O) is the parent-child relation. Projects (offers/collections) own many products; counted via a `groupBy:['project']` aggregate (`projects/api.ts`). `product_link` provides arbitrary product↔product relations (typed `relation_type, direction`), imported from ClickUp.
- **Business units:** `business_unit` exists as a free `string` field on `product`, `project`, and `stage` — this is where "POP Creations" (licensed) vs "Spruce Line" (generic) would be distinguished, but it is **not an enum and not a managed collection**; the UI only displays it as text on the Projects page and never filters POP vs Spruce. "Spruce" does not appear anywhere in the codebase.

**Bottom line:** licensing tracking in poppim today = `product.licensor` (name) + `product.stage` (a column whose name might say "Licensor Review"/"Approved") + free-text `business_unit`. There is no structured submission/approval/Brand-Assurance data model.

## 4. Features & State

**Live / wired to real Directus data:**
- **Pipeline (Kanban board)** — `src/features/pipeline/` — the substance of the app. Real `product` + `stage` data, `@dnd-kit` drag-to-change-stage with optimistic update + revert, server-side search (`name`/`code` `_icontains`) and licensor filter (`_in`), parallel `aggregate` count, debounced 300ms, "showing first N of M" truncation (loads 300 unfiltered / 500 filtered, not all 15K). Table view exists; **Timeline tab is a placeholder** (`AGENTS.md` §15 "open").
- **Projects page** — `src/features/projects/` — real 651 project records + per-project product counts + drill-in to a project's products.
- **Task Detail Modal** — `src/components/TaskDetailModal.tsx` — real assignees, checklist, native comments, plus four work panes (updates, files, fields, activity/links/time) all reading the `product_*` ClickUp-migration collections. Deep-linkable via `?item=<uuid>`.
- **Auth** — email/password + Microsoft SSO redirect; `App.tsx` is a simple auth gate (loading → LoginPage → AppShell). `react-router-dom` is installed but unused (no routes).
- **Cover images** on cards (DO Spaces thumbnails; full original in modal).

**Mock / scaffolding (static data from `src/lib/mockData.ts`, no API):**
- **My Work** (`mywork/`), **Schedule** (`schedule/`), **Notes** (`notes/`), **People** (`people/`), **Settings** (`settings/` — Licensors/Customers tabs with local-only logo upload). Sidebar "Teams" (Design/Sales/Licensing/Production) and "Collections" lists are hardcoded.

**Roles/permissions:** None defined in this repo. Roles are a Directus concept (`DirectusUser.role {id,name}` is read but never used for gating; the People page roles like "Licensing Manager" are mock strings). All permissions/roles live in the `directus` backend repo.

**No AI assistant, no flows/automations, no dashboards, no role-based views** exist in code.

**Completeness:** One genuinely functional module (Pipeline board + task detail + Projects) on real data; the other five nav screens are visual mocks. Per `HANDOFF.md`, production is deployed and SSO redirect verified but full real-tenant SSO round-trip unconfirmed. App is described as effectively preview-stage ("no production incidents — the app is preview-only so far").

## 5. Overlap Surface with DesignFlow PLM's licensing grid

A DesignFlow PLM grid tracking *licensor submissions, products/SKUs, approval status, sample/PPS status, Brand Assurance numbers, factory/vendor, costing/pricing per product* overlaps with poppim **only partially**, and poppim is the weaker model for licensing specifics:

| DesignFlow PLM field | Where it lives in poppim today | Mapping quality |
|---|---|---|
| Product / SKU | `product` collection (`code` = SKU-ish catalog code, `name`, `description`) | Direct — `product.code`/`product.name` |
| Licensor | `product.licensor` → `licensor.name` | Partial — name only, no property/style-guide |
| Property / style guide | — (none) | **No equivalent** |
| Approval status | implied by `product.stage.name` (e.g. "Licensor Review"/"Approved") | Weak — a free-form stage, not a status enum |
| Submission number | — (none) | **No equivalent** |
| Sample / PPS status | — (none; "Sampling" is just a possible stage name) | **No equivalent** |
| Brand Assurance number | — (none) | **No equivalent** |
| Factory / vendor | `MockTask.factory` (mock only) / possibly `product_field` EAV | **No real field** — would land in generic `product_field` |
| Costing / pricing | — (none; only mock note prose mentions price points) | **No equivalent** |
| Retailer / buyer | `product.retailer` → `retailer.name` | Direct |
| Project/offer grouping | `product.project` → `project` | Direct |
| Season | partial — `stage.category`, `business_unit`, "Seasonal" licensor | Weak/free-text |

**Where shared product/submission data would live in poppim:** the `product` row is the natural join key (by `code`/SKU or by `clickup_url`/`linked_external_id` on `product_link`). Any PLM licensing attributes not in poppim's typed schema (submission #, BA #, PPS status, factory, cost) would today only be representable through the generic **`product_field`** EAV collection (`name`/`value_text`/`value_json`) or imported **`product_tag`**s — i.e., unstructured. To make poppim a real licensing tracker matching the PLM grid, the **`directus` backend repo** would need new collections (submission, property/style_guide, factory/vendor, costing) or new `product` fields, and then `src/lib/types.ts` + the pipeline/modal UI extended to surface them. As-is, the concrete overlap is: **product/SKU, licensor (name), retailer/buyer, project grouping, and a coarse stage-as-status** — everything submission/PPS/BA/factory/costing-specific is absent from poppim's data model.
